summary: Do not ship: the authoritative RED record is internally inconsistent, so the mandatory flip proof is not auditable. The runtime change is otherwise scoped and rollback is a ConfigMap revert with automatic vmalert reload.

findings.title: Inconsistent verify-record
findings.body: The lock requires the exact token `ImageDigestDrift did not fire despite`. This record claims `symptomTokenPresent: true`, but its bounded `outputTail` starts at `pite 115 minutes...` and never contains that token. The older record containing it belongs to a superseded red SHA, so it cannot prove the current f4497d2 baseline. This fails the release gate's anti-forgery consistency check.
findings.recommendation: Do not hand-edit the artifact. Make the verifier retain the token-containing line in bounded evidence, rerun `--verify-flip`, commit regenerated RED/GREEN records and refreshed verification, then rerun the release review.

next_steps: Regenerate machine-owned flip evidence with the exact symptom token visible in the RED output tail.
After the gate passes, perform the documented post-sync live checks; rollback requires reverting the rule ConfigMap change and letting vmalert reload it.
